title: Secure GitLab CI pattern for build and deploy
summary: Reference pipeline structure: protected variables, no inline secrets, artifact signing hooks.
body:
A secure pipeline separates build, test, scan, and deploy stages. Use protected CI variables for registry credentials and deployment tokens; never echo secrets in script output. Pin base images by digest in Dockerfile FROM lines where possible.

Add SAST/dependency scanning stages ahead of container build. For GitOps, the deploy job should only apply manifests that passed policy checks and image digest rules—matching AegisML deterministic checks in merge requests.

steps: Define stages: lint, test, build, scan, publish, deploy.
Use only CI variables or vault integration for REGISTRY_USER, REGISTRY_PASSWORD, KUBE_TOKEN.
Fail the pipeline if policy_check.py or equivalent exits non-zero.
remediation.example_manifest_snippet:
stages:
  - test
  - policy
  - build
policy_check:
  stage: policy
  script:
    - cd aegisml && python scripts/policy_check.py
  rules:
    - if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"

nist.name: Security and Privacy Engineering Principles
nist.discussion: Shift-left validation embeds security into the engineering process.

nist.name: Information Input Validation
nist.discussion: Pipeline gates validate inputs (code, config) before promotion.

fedramp.control: CA-2 (Control Assessments) — automated checks as part of CD
fedramp.notes: Document MR policy checks as continuous assessment evidence where applicable.
